COURSE WORKBOOK
with Gretta Van Riel, Rob Ward, Chase Dimond
Richard Li, Nick Shackelford
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

In this lesson, I’m going to teach you how to find
influencers to work with that can leverage an
audience to influence their buying decisions.
This is the most important part in the influencer
marketing process because if you choose the wrong
influencer, you’d be spending time, money and
product without getting conversions in return. Lucky
for you, I’ve distilled this process down to a science.
Influencer + Reach = Brand Exposure
Influencer + Influence = Conversions
Influencers - Influence = Content Creator
The Four Types of Influencers
• 	Nano-Influencers: 1,000-10,000 followers
• 	Micro-Influencer : 10,000-50,000 followers
• 	Macro-Influencers: 500,000+ followers
• 	Mega-Influencers: 1 million + followers
The 3 R’s
Reach x Relevance x Relationships = Influence
Tips for Reach
• 	Look at reach not as a static figure but as a
moving average
• 	To determine reach focus on month-on-month
follower growth
Tips for Relevancy
• 	Understand who your target audience or ideal
customer or customer persona is
• 	Use influencers that have credibility and
authority on the subject matter
LESSON:
IDENTIFYING THE
INFLUENTIAL
MODULE:
ADVANCED INFLUENCER
MARKETING
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	List influencers that would make sense for your
brand for each of the 3 R’s.
❏ 	Use the Social Blade tool linked in the
Resources to find influencers in your niche that
are growing fast.
“Not all self-proclaimed influencers have influence.”
Tips for Relationship
• 	Use engagement as one of the most accurate
indicators of Relationship
• 	Find influencers with almost a cult-like following
• 	Look for influencers who are popular across
multiple platforms
